_note: ROUND 3 (great-grandparents) for the undated_1 batch: 34 round-2 ancestors (grandparent generation, mostly medieval/early-modern European nobility whose round-2 labels carried no parseable year) given THEIR documented parents + notable full siblings, one generation further back. New nodes use the R3K_ prefix; existing tree nodes are referenced by id string. FILLED 21, SKIPPED 13. HIGH-VALUE CROSS-LINKS: (1) Beatrix of Namur (R2E_BEATRIX_NAMUR, this batch) and Alice of Namur (R2F_ALICE_NAMUR, existing round-2 node, Countess of Hainaut) are BOTH daughters of Godfrey I, Count of Namur, & Ermesinde of Luxembourg — one family entry links the two round-2 additions as sisters (brother Henry the Blind added). (2) Marguerite de Bretagne (R2I_MARGUERITE_BRETAGNE) is a documented daughter of John IV, Duke of Brittany, & Joan of Navarre, who ALREADY EXIST as R2H_JOHNIVDUKEBRITTANY + R2H_JOANOFNAVARREENGLAND (added in 41_grandparents_d5.json as parents of Richard, Count of Etampes) — parents referenced by id, Marguerite added as their child (full sister of Richard of Etampes), NOT duplicated. (3) Hildegarde of Burgundy's mother is Ermengarde of Anjou (dau of Fulk III Nerra), who ALREADY EXISTS as SPK_ERMENGARDE_ANJOU (added in 40_spouseparents_u5.json, where her FIRST marriage to Geoffrey II of Gatinais produced Fulk IV); her SECOND marriage, to the new node Robert I, Duke of Burgundy, produced Hildegarde — existing mother referenced by id, linking the Anjou and Burgundy ducal lines. (4) Elizabeth FitzHugh's mother Alice Neville is a daughter of Richard Neville, 5th Earl of Salisbury (existing SPI_RICHARD_SALISBURY) — noted in the family note (Salisbury himself not re-added, one gen further back). NEW DOCUMENTED GRANDLINE LINKS surfaced: Cardinal Jules Mazarin (added as full brother of Laura Margherita Mazzarini) becomes a documented great-granduncle; Robert, Count of Mortain (half-brother of William the Conqueror) enters as Emma of Mortain's father; the Borgia line deepens via Rodrigo de Borja (b.1349) and via Domingo de Borja's union (Domingo's OWN parents skipped as undocumented). FLAGGED disputed/uncertain (source given per note): Regelinda of Zurich's father (Wikipedia: 'Eberhard I, Count of Zurich OR possibly Waltfred, Margrave of Verona and Friuli' — father flagged, mother Gisela named). MODERATE-CONFIDENCE (compilation sources where Wikipedia is silent, flagged in-note): Agnes of Montfaucon's parents (FamilySearch/Geneanet), Bartholomew de l'Ile-Bouchard's parents (geni/WikiTree/Geneanet/FamilySearch cross-agree; he took the L'Ile-Bouchard name via his mother the heiress), Elizabeth Lindsay's mother Marjory Dunbar (thepeerage/geni; father Wikipedia), Mary Langford's parents (History of Parliament/WikiTree), Paola Orsini's parents (geni/Wikidata; she was heiress of Tagliacozzo). SKIPPED 13 (undocumented parentage or unnamed placeholders, no fabrication): R2J_FRANCINALLANCOL & R2J_DOMINGOBORJA (Pope Callixtus III's parents — a minor Valencian merchant/gentry family; their own parents are not documented in Wikipedia or reliable sources); R2D_AVATOURS (her brother Matfrid I, Count of Orleans, is the earliest documented Matfriding — his/their parents are unrecorded, Wikidata lists mother 'unknown'); R2L_BEATRICEMOZZI (Geneanet-only wife of Sozzo de' Bardi, no documented parents); R2L_ELISABETH_CHATEAUDULOIR (compilation-only, no documented parents); R2E_OTHELINDIS_HOLLAND (parentage unresolved between Bernard I, Duke of Saxony [Billung] and Bernard, Margrave of the Nordmark; no scholarly consensus, mother unknown); R2E_HERMANN2_MOTHER (the node itself is a disputed identity — Richlind of Swabia vs Judith of Ohningen — so parents are doubly unresolved); R2D_DONALDII_MOTHER & R2D_OSBURH_MOTHER (unnamed '—' placeholders, no identity to research); R2D_GERBERGERENNES (10th-c. wife of Judicael Berengar of Rennes, parents unrecorded); R2E_ANCILLA_SAVOY (her own identity is uncertain — 'poss. of Lenzburg or of Aoste' — so parentage cannot be assigned); R2I_ISABEL_GODDARD (obscure second wife of John de Wydeville, no documented parents); R2L_GISELA_VERONA (compilation-level figure — Wikipedia 'Judith, Duchess of Bavaria' documents neither Gisela of Verona's nor Eberhard of Sulichgau's parents). 👑 = regnant sovereigns only (dukes/counts/consorts get none, per house style); ✝ = high clergy. Web-verified 2026-07-17 against English/French Wikipedia and, where flagged, genealogical compilations (thepeerage, geni, Geneanet, WikiTree, FamilySearch, History of Parliament).

a.label:
Robert I 'the Old'
1011-1076 · Duke of Burgundy; founder of the elder ducal House of Burgundy · Wikipedia

families.note: Hildegarde of Burgundy (3rd wife of William VIII, Duke of Aquitaine) is documented as the only daughter of Robert I, Duke of Burgundy, by his second wife Ermengarde of Anjou. DEDUP: Ermengarde of Anjou (daughter of Fulk III Nerra of Anjou) ALREADY EXISTS as SPK_ERMENGARDE_ANJOU (40_spouseparents_u5.json), where her FIRST marriage to Geoffrey II, Count of Gatinais, produced Fulk IV of Anjou; her second marriage to Robert I of Burgundy is a separate union — mother referenced by id, linking the Anjou and Burgundy ducal lines. Robert I's children by his set-aside first wife Helie de Semur (incl. Dukes Hugh I and Henry) are half-siblings, excluded. Wikipedia.

families.note: Regelinda (Reginlind) of Zürich (wife of Burchard II, Duke of Swabia, and later of Duke Hermann I) — Wikipedia 'Regelinda of Zürich' names her mother clearly as Gisela (granddaughter of Eberhard of Friuli and the Carolingian Gisela, daughter of Louis the Pious) but flags her FATHER as disputed: 'Eberhard I, Count of Zürich, or possibly Waltfred, Margrave of Verona and Friuli.' The batch label follows the Eberhard identification; the alternative is flagged in the father's label. No documented siblings.
